Records.description: Non-planar (crossed) massless double box with 7 propagators, 4 on-shell legs. Computed via Mellin-Barnes. Result in $\operatorname{Li}_n$ and $S_{n,p}$ through weight 4. Poles from $1/\varepsilon^4$ through $\varepsilon^0$.

Records.authors: Herrmann, Enrico, Parra-Martinez, Julio, Ruf, Michael S., Zeng, Mao
Records.description: Two-loop s-channel crossed double box soft family at $\mathcal{O}(G^3)$. Non-planar crossing of graviton rungs relative to family III.

Records.authors: Bern, Zvi, Enciso, Michael, Ita, Harald, Zeng, Mao
Records.description: Two-loop nonplanar crossed double box with 7 propagators and 2 ISPs, all massless. Nonplanar dual conformal symmetry identified. DEs derived with $\varepsilon$-factorized form. No integral evaluation.

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Caola, Fabrizio, von Manteuffel, Andreas, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Two-loop non-planar integral family for massless $gg \to \gamma\gamma$ with crossed propagator $(k_1-k_2+p_3)^2$.

Records.authors: Argeri, Mario, Di Vita, Stefano, Mastrolia, Pierpaolo, Mirabella, Edoardo, Schlenk, Johannes, Schubert, Ulrich, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Family of 12 uniform-transcendentality master integrals required for the two-loop non-planar (crossed) double box with massless internal lines and four massless on-shell external legs ($p_i^2=0, s+t+u=0$). Sub-topologies $T_a..T_g$ (Fig. Masters3) include the one-loop bubble $T_a$, sunset $T_b$, one-loop box $T_c$, two-loop sunset $T_d$, three-loop banana-like $T_e$, planar double-box $T_f$, and the non-planar crossed double-box $T_g$ (the latter containing an extra $(k_2+p_1)^2$ numerator factor). MIs $f_1..f_9$ coincide with those of the two-loop planar massless double box of Henn arXiv:1304.1806; $f_{10}..f_{12}$ are the new ones needed for the crossed sector. The canonical basis $g_1..g_{12}$ obeys $d g/dx = \varepsilon (M_1/x + M_2/(1-x)) g$ with explicit $12 \times 12$ sparse $M_1, M_2$ given in the paper, expressible in Magnus/Dyson series of HPLs of weight up to 4 in $x=-t/s$. Boundary conditions fixed by direct comparison with Tausk hep-ph/9909506 and Anastasiou-Gehrmann-Oleari-Remiddi-Tausk hep-ph/0003261.
Records.location: Section 6 (xbox2L.tex), Eqs. (6.1)-(6.7), Figs. Diagrams3, Masters3; results in Appendix MIxbox.tex

Records.authors: Bosma, Jorrit, Larsen, Kasper J., Zhang, Yang
Records.description: Fully massless non-planar double-box integral family with 7 propagators and 2 ISPs. Second worked example demonstrating enhanced ideal membership and canonical differential equations in Baikov representation without squared propagators. Canonical form with dlog alphabet $\{\chi, \chi+1\}$ obtained via Fuchsia, where $\chi = t/s$.

Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Johansson, Henrik, Kälin, Gregor, Mogull, Gustav, Verbeek, Bram
Records.description: Non-planar (crossed) double-box topology for the two-loop four-gluon amplitude in $\mathcal{N}=2$ SQCD. Diagrams (f), (g), (h) share this non-planar propagator structure with pure adjoint, pure fundamental, and mixed particle content. Numerators determined from planar ones via color-kinematics duality. Propagator denominators defined in Ref. [Johansson:2017bfl]. Diagram (f) contributes only to $\mathcal{N}=4$ SYM part; (g) and (h) contribute to the remainder $R$.

Two-loop massless nonplanar (crossed) double box with four massless external legs; hepta-cut residues computed for integrals with doubled and tripled propagators; scalar integral basis proposed eliminating tensor integrals

Records.authors: De Angelis, Stefano, Kosower, David A., Ma, Rourou, Wu, Zihao, Zhang, Yang
Records.description: Called NP in the paper. Nonplanar (crossed) double box with 7 propagators + 2 ISPs. 12-element singularity-free basis. Application to subleading-color two-loop all-plus four-gluon amplitude.

Records.authors: Anastasiou, C., Gehrmann, T., Oleari, C., Remiddi, E., Tausk, J.B.
Records.description: Two-loop massless non-planar (crossed) double box with four light-like external legs. Seven propagators and two ISPs. Reduced to two master integrals via IBP and Lorentz-invariance identities. Analytic $\varepsilon$ expansion through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$ in terms of polylogarithms and Nielsen generalized polylogarithms.

Records.authors: C. Anastasiou, T. Gehrmann, C. Oleari, E. Remiddi, J.B. Tausk
Records.description: The authors give relations derived from the IBP identities to reduce any massless on-shell crossed double box to master integrals. Amongst these master integrals, the authors compute the only one missing that does not have simpler topology.

Records.authors: Anastasiou, C., Gehrmann, T., Oleari, C., Remiddi, E., Tausk, J.B.
Records.description: Two-loop massless non-planar (crossed) double box with four light-like external legs. Seven propagators and two ISPs. Reduced to two master integrals via IBP and Lorentz-invariance identities. Analytic epsilon expansion through O(eps^0) in terms of polylogarithms and Nielsen generalized polylogarithms.

Records.authors: Gambuti, Giulio, Kosower, David A., Novichkov, Pavel P., Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Massless non-planar double box, featuring a non-subtraction-free second Symanzik polynomial and power-like soft divergences. A subtraction-free linear combination of the Landau equations is constructed to prove no cancellation-induced singularities arise. The truncated ideal of locally finite numerators has 17 generators (9 at rank 3, 8 at rank 4), producing 230 finite integrals up to rank 5. One evanescent generator at rank 4.
Records.location: Sec. 7.3, Eqs. (7.22)-(7.30), Table VI, Figs. 8-9, Appendix A.2

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Frellesvig, Hjalte, Marzucca, Robin, Morales, Roger, Seefeld, Florian, Wilhelm, Matthias, Yang, Tong-Zhi
Records.description: $I^{**}_{3,2,2}$ two-loop topology with three internal paths of lengths a=3, b=2, c=2 (neither v_L nor v_R carries external legs); 4-point integral, non-planar (b=>=2). Geometry: 1 ISP; max cut ∫dz/(√Q_2(z)·√P_4(z)); genus-2 hyperelliptic (§3.4 Type 1.4)

Records.authors: Fevola, C., Mizera, S., Telen, S.
Records.description: Landau singularities of the non-planar double box computed via principal Landau determinants.
